DevOps Fundamentals.aiRubric: Check basic bash commands, networking concepts, cloud provider basics, IAM setup, and CI/CD logic.

lessons.title: Linux Command Line & Bash Scripting
lessons.theory:
## Bash Scripting
Bash is the default shell for many Linux distros.
```bash
#!/bin/bash
echo "Starting script..."
ls -la /var/www
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Simple Script
Write a bash script that prints 'Hello DevOps' and then lists the contents of the current directory.
lessons.rubric: echo and ls commands used correctly.

lessons.title: Networking Basics (IP, DNS, HTTP)
lessons.theory:
## DNS & HTTP
DNS resolves domains to IPs. HTTP is the protocol of the web.
```bash
curl -I https://api.github.com
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Curl Headers
Use `curl` to fetch only the HTTP headers from `http://example.com`.

lessons.title: Introduction to Cloud Providers (AWS & Azure)
lessons.theory:
## Cloud Providers
AWS uses CLI commands like `aws s3 ls`.
```bash
aws ec2 describe-instances
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: AWS CLI
Write the AWS CLI command to list all S3 buckets.

lessons.title: Identity & Access Management (IAM)
lessons.theory:
## IAM Policies
IAM defines permissions using JSON policies.
```json
{
  "Effect": "Allow",
  "Action": "s3:ListBucket",
  "Resource": "*"
}
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: IAM JSON
Complete the IAM policy to `Allow` the action `ec2:DescribeInstances`.

lessons.title: CI/CD Pipelines (GitHub Actions)
lessons.theory:
## GitHub Actions
Automate workflows using YAML.
```yaml
name: CI
on: [push]
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Trigger Action
Configure the GitHub Action workflow to trigger `on: [pull_request]`.
lessons.starterCode:
name: CI
on: [___]
jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
lessons.solution:
name: CI
on: [pull_request]
jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

lessons.title: Core Concepts of DevOps Fundamentals
lessons.theory: ## Understanding DevOps Fundamentals\nBefore diving into complex implementations, it's essential to understand the foundational principles of DevOps Fundamentals.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Define Core Concept\n1. Create a function `get_concept()` that returns the string 'DevOps Fundamentals Foundation'.
lessons.hint: Return the exact string 'DevOps Fundamentals Foundation'.
lessons.rubric: Function returns the correct concept string.

lessons.title: Setting up the Environment for DevOps Fundamentals
lessons.theory: ## Tooling\nProperly configuring your development environment is the first step in mastering DevOps Fundamentals.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Environment Status\n1. Create a function `is_ready()` that returns True.

lessons.title: Basic Syntax and Operations
lessons.theory: ## Syntax Rules\nEvery discipline has its syntax. Here we apply standard operations relevant to DevOps Fundamentals.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Basic Math\n1. Return the sum of a and b.
lessons.starterCode: def add(a, b):\n    return a ___ b
lessons.solution: def add(a, b):\n    return a + b

lessons.title: Control Structures in DevOps Fundamentals
lessons.theory: ## Branching\nConditional logic dictates how applications respond to different states.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Check State\n1. Return 'Active' if state is 1, else 'Inactive'.

lessons.title: Advanced Memory Management
lessons.theory: ## Resource Allocation\nEfficient memory use is critical, especially when applying DevOps Fundamentals at scale.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Clear Memory\n1. Set the variable `cache` to an empty dictionary.

lessons.title: Object-Oriented Approaches
lessons.theory: ## OOP\nEncapsulating state and behavior within objects helps structure DevOps Fundamentals projects.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Create Class\n1. Create a class `Module` with a property `name` initialized to 'DevOps Fundamentals'.

lessons.title: Functional Programming Paradigms
lessons.theory: ## Pure Functions\nFunctional programming avoids side effects. This is increasingly popular in DevOps Fundamentals.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Pure Function\n1. Write a function `square(x)` that returns x squared.

lessons.title: Error Handling and Debugging
lessons.theory: ## Resilience\nHandling exceptions gracefully ensures robustness in DevOps Fundamentals.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Try/Except\n1. Wrap the division in a try block, catch ZeroDivisionError, and return None.

lessons.title: Advanced Debugging in DevOps Fundamentals
lessons.theory: ## Debugging at Scale\nFinding hard-to-reproduce bugs requires structured methodology.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Debug Setup\n1. Return True for debugging active.

lessons.title: Profiling DevOps Fundamentals Applications
lessons.theory: ## Performance Profiling\nIdentifying bottlenecks is the first step in optimization.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Profile Scope\n1. Set `profile_mode` to 'High'.

lessons.title: Memory Leaks and Management
lessons.theory: ## Garbage Collection\nUnderstanding how memory is freed is critical.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: GC Status\n1. Set `gc_enabled` to True.

lessons.title: Multi-threading Constructs
lessons.theory: ## Parallelism\nUtilizing multiple cores improves execution speed.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Thread Count\n1. Set `threads` to 8.

lessons.title: Final Project: DevOps Fundamentals
lessons.theory: ## Capstone\nApply all learned concepts to build a comprehensive solution.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Project Initialization\n1. Set `project_name` to 'DevOps Fundamentals Final'.

Containers & Infrastructure.aiRubric: Check Docker syntax, Docker Compose yaml structure, Terraform HCL logic, and Ansible playbooks.

lessons.title: Introduction to Docker & Containerization
lessons.theory:
## Dockerfiles
Define container images.
```dockerfile
FROM node:18
COPY . /app
CMD ["node", "app.js"]
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Docker FROM
Start a Dockerfile using the `python:3.9-slim` image and set the working directory to `/app`.

lessons.title: Docker Compose & Multi-Container Apps
lessons.theory:
## Docker Compose
Run multi-container applications via YAML.
```yaml
services:
  db:
    image: postgres
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Compose Service
Define a service named `web` using the `nginx:latest` image mapping port `8080:80`.

lessons.title: Infrastructure as Code (IaC) Concepts
lessons.theory:
## IaC
Manage infrastructure using declarative code. Tools include Terraform, Pulumi, and CloudFormation.
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Principles
Define the core benefit of IaC by assigning `true` to `isDeclarative`.

lessons.title: Provisioning with Terraform
lessons.theory:
## Terraform HCL
Use HashiCorp Configuration Language to provision resources.
```hcl
resource "aws_s3_bucket" "bucket" {
  bucket = "my-bucket"
}
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: AWS EC2 Resource
Define an `aws_instance` resource named `web_server` with an ami of `ami-12345`.

lessons.title: Configuration Management with Ansible
lessons.theory:
## Ansible Playbooks
Automate configuration via SSH.
```yaml
- hosts: all
  tasks:
    - name: Ensure nginx is installed
      apt: name=nginx state=present
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Ansible Task
Write a task using the `apt` module to install `git` (state=present).
lessons.starterCode:
- hosts: webservers
  tasks:
    - name: Install Git
      ___: name=___ state=___
lessons.solution:
- hosts: webservers
  tasks:
    - name: Install Git
      apt: name=git state=present

lessons.title: Concurrency and Async Operations
lessons.theory: ## Asynchronous Work\nBlocking operations freeze applications. Async allows overlapping tasks in Containers & Infrastructure.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Async Function\n1. Define an async function `fetch_data()`.

lessons.title: Working with Data Structures
lessons.theory: ## Data Organization\nChoosing the right data structure dictates performance.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Use a Set\n1. Return a set containing the elements 1, 2, 3.

lessons.title: Network Communication Protocols
lessons.theory: ## Connectivity\nUnderstanding HTTP/TCP is essential for networked components in Containers & Infrastructure.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: HTTP Status\n1. Return 200 for OK.

lessons.title: File I/O and Storage
lessons.theory: ## Persistence\nSaving data to disk allows state to survive reboots.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Open File\n1. Write code to open 'data.txt' in read mode.

lessons.title: Security Best Practices
lessons.theory: ## Defense\nProtecting applications from vulnerabilities is non-negotiable.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Hash Length\n1. Return 64, the length of a SHA-256 hash in hex.

lessons.title: Performance Optimization Strategies
lessons.theory: ## Efficiency\nOptimizing critical paths improves throughput in Containers & Infrastructure.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Time Complexity\n1. Return 'O(1)' for constant time complexity.

lessons.title: Unit Testing Fundamentals
lessons.theory: ## Validation\nUnit tests verify that individual components work as expected.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Assert True\n1. Write an assert statement that 1 equals 1.

lessons.title: Integration Testing
lessons.theory: ## System Harmony\nIntegration tests ensure that components function together.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Integration Status\n1. Set `integrated` to True.

Orchestration & Observability.aiRubric: Check Kubernetes YAML (pods, deployments, services), ArgoCD concepts, and Prometheus/ELK configurations.

lessons.title: Introduction to Kubernetes (K8s) Architecture
lessons.theory:
## Kubernetes
Orchestrates containers across clusters.
```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Pod YAML
Complete the YAML to define a `Pod` with the name `my-app`.
lessons.solution:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: my-app

lessons.title: Deploying Pods & Services in Kubernetes
lessons.theory:
## Services
Expose Pods to the network.
```yaml
kind: Service
spec:
  selector:
    app: my-app
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Service Selector
Match the Service selector to the app label `backend` on port `80`.
lessons.starterCode:
kind: Service
spec:
  ___:
    app: ___
  ports:
    - port: ___
lessons.solution:
kind: Service
spec:
  selector:
    app: backend
  ports:
    - port: 80
lessons.rubric: K8s Service selector and port specified.

lessons.title: Continuous Deployment (GitOps with ArgoCD)
lessons.theory:
## GitOps
Store the desired state in Git. ArgoCD syncs the cluster to match Git.
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Argo Application
Set the `project` to `default` and the `repoURL` to `https://github.com/my/repo`.
lessons.starterCode:
apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: Application
spec:
  project: ___
  source:
    repoURL: ___
lessons.solution:
apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: Application
spec:
  project: default
  source:
    repoURL: https://github.com/my/repo

lessons.title: Monitoring with Prometheus & Grafana
lessons.theory:
## Prometheus
Scrapes metrics from /metrics endpoints.
```yaml
scrape_configs:
  - job_name: 'api'
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Scrape Config
Set the `job_name` to `'prometheus'` and the `static_configs` target to `localhost:9090`.

lessons.title: Centralized Logging (ELK Stack)
lessons.theory:
## ELK
Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana.
```json
GET /logs/_search
{ "query": { "match": { "level": "error" } } }
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Elastic Query
Write the Elasticsearch JSON query to match the `status` field with `500`.

lessons.title: Continuous Integration Setup
lessons.theory: ## CI Pipelines\nCI automatically runs tests when code is pushed.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Pipeline Step\n1. Create a list with 'build', 'test', 'deploy'.

lessons.title: Deployment Pipelines
lessons.theory: ## Shipping Code\nDeploying reliably requires automated release pipelines.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Environment\n1. Set `env` to 'production'.

lessons.title: Logging and Monitoring
lessons.theory: ## Observability\nLogs help diagnose issues in production.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Log Level\n1. Set `level` to 'ERROR'.

lessons.title: Design Patterns in Orchestration & Observability
lessons.theory: ## Reusable Solutions\nDesign patterns solve common architectural problems.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Singleton\n1. Set `pattern` to 'Singleton'.

lessons.title: API Integration Strategies
lessons.theory: ## Connecting Services\nAPIs allow different systems to communicate.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: JSON Data\n1. Set `format` to 'JSON'.

lessons.title: Database Connectivity
lessons.theory: ## Data Access\nConnecting to a DB safely is essential.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Connection String\n1. Set `conn_str` to 'postgres://localhost'.

lessons.title: Scaling Applications
lessons.theory: ## Horizontal Scaling\nAdding more instances handles increased load.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Instance Count\n1. Set `instances` to 3.

lessons.title: Microservices Architecture
lessons.theory: ## Decoupling\nMicroservices break large apps into independent services.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Service Name\n1. Set `service` to 'Auth'.

lessons.title: Best Practices and Anti-patterns
lessons.theory: ## Writing Clean Code\nMaintainability ensures the long-term success of Orchestration & Observability projects.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Clean Code\n1. Set `is_clean` to True.
